title: Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple
tagline: Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: A Hindu temple situated in a small hill called Aananda Giri Hill near Hebbal, Bangalore capital city of Karnataka state, India, was originated in 13th century Chola dynasty era.
overview.description: A Hindu temple situated in a small hill called Aananda Giri Hill near Hebbal, Bangalore capital city of Karnataka state, India, was originated in 13th century Chola dynasty era. The temple was renovated in 2000 (the temple itself), 2009 (surrounding places of the temple), and 2012 (Ganesha Temple on the west side of the hill). In 2013 a Kala Bairaveshwara idol was installed in a cave towards east of the hill.

topPlaces.name: Hebbal-Kittayya inscription
topPlaces.description: Hebbal is a locality situated in the northern part of Bengaluru, is recognized as one of Bengaluru’s oldest regions, with documented history extending back at least 1,300 years. Originally marking the northern boundary of the city, Bengaluru has since expanded significantly northward.

topPlaces.name: Hebbal, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Hebbal is a neighbourhood in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, which was once indicative of the north endpoint of the city. Though originally famous for Hebbal Lake, it is now better known for the serpentine maze of flyovers that network the Outer Ring Road and Airport Road on NH 44.

topPlaces.name: Hebbala metro station
topPlaces.description: Hebbala is an upcoming elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Namma Metro in Bangalore, India. This is a prime location for its neighbouring areas like Hebbal and Kempapura.

topPlaces.name: Hebbal Lake, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Hebbal Lake is located in the north of Bangalore at the mouth of National Highway 7, along the junction of Bellary Road and the Outer Ring Road (ORR). Epigraphic evidence suggests that the lake is much older than commonly assumed.

topPlaces.name: Sanjaynagar
topPlaces.description: Sanjaynagar is a residential area located in northern Bangalore, India. Established in the late 1970s, it is now a busy and sprawling locality in Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Bhoopasandra inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: The Bhoopasandra inscriptions and hero stones are found in Bhoopasandra, a sub-locality in Hebbal, Bengaluru. It is the home to an idol of goddess Durga with a Kannada inscription dated to the 8th century CE, the oldest found in Bengaluru with a documented history of more than a millennium.

topPlaces.name: Ganganagar, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Ganganagar is one of the neighbourhoods of Bengaluru. It is in the north of Bengaluru and is on the east of NH-7 before Hebbal.

topPlaces.name: Kodigehalli metro station
topPlaces.description: Kodigehalli is an upcoming important elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Namma Metro in Bangalore, India. This metro station will mainly serve the Brigade Corporate Offices, consisting of Magnum, Opus, Triumph and Caladium.

items.note: Hebbal is a locality situated in the northern part of Bengaluru, is recognized as one of Bengaluru’s oldest regions, with documented history extending back at least 1,300 years. Originally marking the northern boundary of the city, Bengaluru has since expanded significantly northward.

Hebbal is a neighbourhood in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, which was once indicative of the north endpoint of the city. Though originally famous for Hebbal Lake, it is now better known for the serpentine maze of flyovers that network the Outer Ring Road and Airport Road on NH 44.

Hebbala is an upcoming elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Namma Metro in Bangalore, India. This is a prime location for its neighbouring areas like Hebbal and Kempapura.

Hebbal Lake is located in the north of Bangalore at the mouth of National Highway 7, along the junction of Bellary Road and the Outer Ring Road (ORR). Epigraphic evidence suggests that the lake is much older than commonly assumed.
Sanjaynagar is a residential area located in northern Bangalore, India. Established in the late 1970s, it is now a busy and sprawling locality in Bangalore.

The Bhoopasandra inscriptions and hero stones are found in Bhoopasandra, a sub-locality in Hebbal, Bengaluru. It is the home to an idol of goddess Durga with a Kannada inscription dated to the 8th century CE, the oldest found in Bengaluru with a documented history of more than a millennium.
Ganganagar is one of the neighbourhoods of Bengaluru. It is in the north of Bengaluru and is on the east of NH-7 before Hebbal.

Kodigehalli is an upcoming important elevated metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Namma Metro in Bangalore, India. This metro station will mainly serve the Brigade Corporate Offices, consisting of Magnum, Opus, Triumph and Caladium.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (22 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Ananda Lingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hebbal-Kittayya inscription, Hebbal, Bengaluru, Hebbala metro station, Hebbal Lake, Bengaluru, Sanjaynagar.
